title: cupressus goveniana var. cornuta
intro: Cupressus goveniana var. cornuta, also known as the Incense Cedar, is a plant species that belongs to the cypress family and is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Cupressus goveniana var. cornuta include reducing stress and anxiety, as well as acting as a natural insect repellent. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, teas, and incense sticks, which can be used to promote relaxation and well-being.
